title: LLM Logbook Schema (General Projects)
description: Schema for tracking context across sessions in non-software projects (academic, creative, business, other). Supports long-term memory, objective tracking with actionable guidance, and progress documentation.

ticket.description: Metadata of the task, milestone, or deliverable that anchors this logbook. This is the ultimate goal that all objectives contribute to.
ticket.$comment: Populate from user input. Title and description are required. URL is optional (link to project management tool, shared document, etc.).

title.description: Title of the task, milestone, or deliverable. Should be concise and descriptive.
title.$comment: Ask user for clear title. Keep under 100 characters.

url.description: URL to related resource (project management tool, shared document, reference material).
url.$comment: Optional. Leave empty string if no external resource. Examples: Notion page, Google Doc, Trello card.

description.description: Detailed explanation of what needs to be accomplished. Includes acceptance criteria, deadlines, or constraints if available.
description.$comment: Ask user to explain thoroughly. Include: deliverables, deadlines, stakeholders, constraints.

objectives.description: Project objectives organized by priority. Main objectives define high-level deliverables. Secondary objectives are actionable steps with guidance.
objectives.$comment: Main objectives provide context and references. Secondary objectives provide step-by-step guidance. Both can be populated together.

main.description: Primary objectives that define major deliverables or milestones.
main.$comment: Each main objective should have clear context and reference materials. For academic: chapters, papers. For creative: assets, deliverables. For business: milestones, documents.

secondary.description: Actionable objectives that contribute to main objectives. Each contains specific guidance for completion.
secondary.$comment: Break down main objectives into completable steps. Provide guidance referencing documents, examples, standards, or best practices.

recent_context.description: Running log of recent interactions, findings, decisions, and progress. Newest entries at index 0. Sliding window that compacts to history_summary when full.
recent_context.$comment: Prepend new entries at index 0. When array exceeds maxItems, remove last (oldest) entry and summarize it to history_summary. Never replace entire array.

items.description: Single context entry representing a meaningful interaction, finding, or decision.
items.$comment: Create entry for: progress updates, important findings, decisions made, blockers encountered, feedback received. Keep content focused and actionable.

id.description: Unique identifier for this entry. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment by 1 globally across all entries. Never reuse IDs.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when entry was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Entry content describing action, decision, finding, or progress. Should be meaningful and provide context for future sessions.
content.$comment: Write concisely but completely. Include: what happened, why it matters, any decisions made. Future sessions will use this to understand context.

mood.description: User's emotional state during this entry if observable (focused, frustrated, excited, uncertain, overwhelmed).
mood.$comment: Optional. Only populate if user mood is clearly observable. Helps adjust communication style.

history_summary.description: Compact summaries of entries removed from recent_context. Preserves continuity when short-term memory is full. Newest at index 0.
history_summary.$comment: Write here ONLY when truncating recent_context. Summarize removed entry in max 140 chars. Prepend at index 0. If exceeds maxItems, remove oldest (last) entry.

items.description: Compressed summary of a removed recent_context entry.
items.$comment: Capture essential information only: what was done, key decision, or important finding.

id.description: Unique identifier for this summary. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment by 1. Separate sequence from recent_context IDs.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when summary was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Compressed summary of removed entry. Must capture essential information.
content.$comment: Summarize in tweet-length. Focus on: what was done, outcome, or key decision.

mood.description: User's emotional state at time of original entry if it was recorded.
mood.$comment: Optional. Preserve from original entry if present.

future_reminders.description: User-created reminders for future sessions. Agent surfaces these when their time arrives.
future_reminders.$comment: Only add reminders when user explicitly requests. Check this array at session start and surface any due reminders.

items.description: A scheduled reminder to surface in a future session.
items.$comment: Create only on explicit user request. Remove after surfacing to user.

id.description: Unique identifier for this reminder. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment by 1. Never reuse IDs.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when reminder was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Reminder message to surface to user when due.
content.$comment: Write exactly what user wants to be reminded about. Include any context needed.

when.description: UTC timestamp when this reminder should be surfaced to user.
when.$comment: Compare against current time at session start. Surface if current time >= when.

main_objective.description: High-level objective defining a major deliverable or milestone. Provides context and reference materials for deeper work.
main_objective.$comment: Define clear outcomes with supporting context. References can be documents, URLs, assets, or any relevant materials. Standards can be style guides, regulations, methodologies, or best practices.

id.description: Unique identifier for this main objective. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment by 1. Never reuse IDs even after abandonment.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when objective was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Verifiable outcome statement that serves as acceptance criteria. When this statement is true, the objective is achieved.
content.$comment: Write as outcome, not task. Validate: Can you clearly determine if this is done? If not, rewrite to be more specific.

context.description: Rationale and background: who needs this, what problem it solves, deadlines, dependencies, or constraints.
context.$comment: Include information that helps make decisions. Reference stakeholders, deadlines, dependencies, or quality standards.

scope.description: Boundaries for this objective: reference materials and standards/guidelines to follow.
scope.$comment: References are starting points (documents, URLs, assets, examples). Standards are guidelines (style guides, regulations, methodologies, best practices).

references.description: Reference materials for this objective: documents, URLs, assets, examples, or prior work to build upon.
references.$comment: List relevant materials. Examples: 'Chapter 2 draft in Google Docs', 'Competitor analysis PDF', 'Brand guidelines v2', 'client_feedback_notes.md', 'https://example.com/reference'.

standards.description: Guidelines, standards, or methodologies to follow. Empty array if none defined.
standards.$comment: List applicable standards. Examples: 'APA 7th edition citation format', 'Brand voice guidelines', 'ISO 27001 compliance', 'Lean Canvas methodology'.

status.description: Current state: not_started (pending work), active (in progress), blocked (waiting on external input), achieved (completed), abandoned (cancelled).
status.$comment: Set only ONE main objective as 'active' at a time. Use 'blocked' when waiting for feedback, resources, or dependencies. Set 'achieved' when content statement is verified true.

secondary_objective.description: Actionable objective that contributes to a main objective. Contains specific guidance for completion.
secondary_objective.$comment: Break work into completable steps. Provide guidance referencing documents, examples, standards, or established practices. Each should be achievable in a focused work session.

id.description: Unique identifier across all secondary objectives. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment globally across all secondary objectives. Never reuse IDs.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when objective was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Specific verifiable outcome that contributes to task completion. Should be completable in a focused work session.
content.$comment: Be granular. Validate: Can this be completed and verified in one session? If not, split into multiple objectives.

completion_guide.description: Step-by-step guidance for completing this objective. References documents, examples, standards, or established practices.
completion_guide.$comment: Each item should provide actionable guidance. Reference specific documents, sections, examples, or standards. Examples: 'Follow structure from Chapter 1 introduction', 'Use client feedback from meeting notes dated Jan 15', 'Apply APA format for all citations', 'Reference competitor X landing page for layout inspiration'.

status.description: Current state: not_started (pending work), active (in progress), blocked (waiting on external input), achieved (completed), abandoned (cancelled).
status.$comment: Set only ONE secondary objective as 'active' at a time. Work sequentially. Set 'achieved' after verifying content statement is true.
